key_insight: Conforming finite-element matrices can be cast in a local Gram/SPSD form, enabling a black-box multilevel algebraic preconditioner (LS-AMG-DD) built directly from element-wise energy structure.
engineering_relevance: This offers a practical preconditioning route for difficult SPD finite-element systems in H(div), H^2, and vector H^1 settings without requiring problem-specific AMG tuning.
method: Identify a cover of degrees of freedom and verify a local SPSD splitting of the stiffness matrix.
Build local spectral problems from the Gram factors to construct interpolation P.
Form coarse operators algebraically via Galerkin coarsening with G_c = GP and A_c = G_c^T G_c.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports numerical tests where LS-AMG-DD outperformed classical AMG on challenging problems, including cases where classical AMG needed more than 10^5 iterations and some comparisons showed 2–5 orders of magnitude smaller errors at the same residual tolerance.
watch_item: The approach depends on obtaining a valid local Gram/SPSD factorization on the chosen cover, so implementation may be sensitive to how local factors are constructed for each finite-element block.
paper_title: A black-box, multilevel algebraic preconditioning framework for conforming finite elements
